critiques.title: Melatonin-Induced Diatoxanthin Accumulation as Thermal NPQ Buffer
key_concerns: MAPK pathway poorly characterized in dinoflagellates
Must control for direct ROS scavenging confound
critic_questions: What specific mechanism enhances xanthophyll cycle? MAPK, lumen pH, or cyclic electron flow?

critiques.title: AFMK-AMK Cascade Compensates for GSH Crash
critiques.kill_reason: Boutin 2024 (J Pineal Res) demonstrates direct scavenging requires 10,000-100,000x physiological concentrations. Cascade stoichiometry only proven in vitro. 215 uM from Gonyaulax cold stress not transferable to Symbiodiniaceae heat stress.

critiques.title: Nocturnal Antioxidant Pre-Loading Determines Resilience
key_concerns: Post-transcriptional gene regulation dominance in dinoflagellates
D1 repair alternative for night-heat effect
strengths_retained: Nocturnal melatonin timing confirmed by Roopin 2013
critic_questions: How to account for post-transcriptional regulation dominance?
Can mechanism be reframed around translational regulation?

critiques.title: Melatonin as Symbiont-to-Host Immune Signal
critiques.kill_reason: >50% mechanism claims unverifiable. No evidence melatonin exits symbiosome at meaningful concentrations. Melatonin-NF-kB is context-dependent. Simpler signaling molecules already proposed.

critiques.title: TPH/AANAT Expression Predicts Thermal Tolerance
strengths_retained: Directly testable with existing public data (PRJNA723630)
critic_questions: Plan if TPH/AANAT too divergent for BLAST? Use HMM profiles?

critiques.title: IDO-Mediated Tryptophan Diversion
critiques.kill_reason: IDO does not exist in any dinoflagellate species. STRING score 0.931 is for HUMAN IDO1-AANAT. Km values extrapolated across ~1 billion years divergence. Entire mechanism built on unverifiable foundation.
key_concerns: Bridge mechanism nonexistent in target organism

critiques.title: D1 Repair Cycle Acceleration by Melatonin
survival_conditions: Must include lincomycin disambiguation test to separate D1 repair acceleration from ROS scavenging
key_concerns: Plant D1 repair may differ from dinoflagellate
critic_questions: How to distinguish D1 repair enhancement from general ROS scavenging?

critiques.title: Genus-Specific Melatonin Drives Symbiont Shuffling
critiques.kill_reason: Differential mortality is simpler explanation. Extracellular melatonin diluted below signaling threshold. Host cannot detect cell-level gradients. All key claims parametric/unverifiable.

kill_reasons: H2: Boutin 2024 counter-evidence invalidates direct scavenging mechanism
H4: >50% unverifiable claims, simpler alternatives exist
H6: IDO does not exist in dinoflagellates — bridge mechanism nonexistent
H8: Simpler alternative (differential mortality) + physical implausibility
critic_questions: H1: What specific mechanism enhances xanthophyll cycle — MAPK, lumen pH, or cyclic electron flow?
H3: How to account for post-transcriptional regulation dominance in dinoflagellates?
H7: How to experimentally distinguish D1 repair enhancement from general ROS scavenging?
H5: Plan if TPH/AANAT too divergent for BLAST — use HMM profiles?

meta_critique.calibration: Calibrated — each kill has specific fatal flaw, each survivor has identified weaknesses
meta_critique.key_discovery: Boutin 2024 (J Pineal Res) challenges direct melatonin ROS scavenging in vivo — paradigm shift from stoichiometric to signaling mechanism
